text.title: PMBoK 7 Reference Tables
text.summary: PMBoK 7 eight performance domains, twelve principles, EVM formulae, estimation accuracy bands, risk response strategies, and RAG thresholds.

list.item: Stakeholder — engaging stakeholders; outcome: agreement on objectives and sustained support
Team — building high-performing teams; outcome: shared ownership and growth
Development Approach — selecting predictive/agile/hybrid; outcome: right approach for context
Planning — scope, schedule, cost, resources; outcome: realistic and achievable plans
Project Work — executing and monitoring; outcome: efficient delivery
Delivery — value and outcomes; outcome: benefits realised
Measurement — tracking with metrics; outcome: data-driven decisions
Uncertainty — risks and complexity; outcome: managed uncertainty
P-01 Stewardship: act with integrity, be accountable
P-02 Team: build culture of accountability and respect
P-03 Stakeholders: engage proactively
P-04 Value: focus on outcomes, not outputs
P-05 Systems Thinking: see the whole, understand interdependencies
P-06 Leadership: inspire, adapt style, enable others
P-07 Tailoring: adapt processes to context
P-08 Quality: build quality into processes
P-09 Complexity: navigate with appropriate strategies
P-10 Risk: balance risk with reward
P-11 Adaptability: embrace change, learn from experience
P-12 Change: enable envisioned future state

rule.statement: Always show formula AND result when reporting EVM metrics. Never emit only the computed value — auditors need the formula to verify the calculation.
rule.rationale: Audit trails require reproducibility. A bare "$92K EAC" without formula cannot be verified or challenged.

rule.statement: Clamp EVM denominators to avoid division by zero. When PV or AC is zero, emit null for the affected metrics rather than infinity or NaN.
rule.rationale: LLM arithmetic on EVM rows with zero or near-zero denominators produces nonsense results that look plausible. Code calculation with explicit guards is required.

list.item: SV (Schedule Variance) = EV - PV; positive = ahead of schedule
CV (Cost Variance) = EV - AC; positive = under budget
SPI (Schedule Performance Index) = EV / PV; greater than 1 = ahead
CPI (Cost Performance Index) = EV / AC; greater than 1 = under budget
EAC (Estimate at Completion) = BAC / CPI
ETC (Estimate to Complete) = EAC - AC
VAC (Variance at Completion) = BAC - EAC
Analogous: -50% to +100% — early stages, rough order of magnitude
Parametric: -10% to +25% — historical data available
Bottom-up: -5% to +10% — detailed scope defined, most accurate
Three-point (PERT): variable — risk-aware, useful with high uncertainty

rule.statement: Present estimation accuracy bands as heuristics with ranges, never as guarantees. An agent that quotes "+/- 10%" without "bottom-up only" misleads sponsors.
rule.rationale: Sponsors anchor on the tightest accuracy band regardless of which technique was used. The technique must be named alongside the band.

list.item: Threats: Avoid (eliminate), Mitigate (reduce probability/impact), Transfer (shift to third party), Accept (acknowledge and monitor)
Opportunities: Exploit (ensure occurrence), Enhance (increase probability/impact), Share (partner to capture), Accept (take if occurs)

rule.statement: Always pair a risk entry with exactly one response strategy. A risk without a strategy is an observation, not a managed risk.
rule.rationale: Risk registers that lack strategies are decoration. The strategy and owner are what make a risk register actionable in execution.

list.item: Green: on track — continue monitoring
Amber: at risk — corrective action plan required within 48 hours
Red: off track — escalation required immediately

rule.statement: If more than 50% of projects in a portfolio are amber, the RAG threshold is miscalibrated, not the projects. Recalibrate before acting on individual project statuses.
rule.rationale: RAG inflation (everyone is amber) destroys the signal. Amber must be rare enough that it triggers genuine attention.
